meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve no version control (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Mastering Version Control for AI Projects
content.seo_description: Stop losing your code. Learn how to implement professional project management and version control using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert guide.
content.answer_block: Without version control, your codebase is a ticking time bomb of lost progress. By integrating structured project management with Git-based versioning via Claude Code and Antigravity, you transform chaotic files into a professional, scalable repository. This workflow ensures every experimental feature is tracked, reversible, and ready for your portfolio.
content.intro:
You’ve spent hours crafting the perfect logic for your first AI app, only to realize you’ve overwritten your only working copy. The dread of 'code rot' and lost progress is the rite of passage for every developer, but it doesn't have to be yours. 

By treating your coding environment as a structured project rather than a folder of scripts, you gain the superpower of 'time travel'—the ability to revert to any working state. This guide bridges the gap between hacking code and engineering software using professional-grade tools.

steps.title: Initialize Your Repository
steps.description: Establish a secure Git foundation to track every character change in your codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current directory and initialize a professional .gitignore file for a Python-based AI project, ensuring all virtual environments and secrets are excluded.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && git add . && git commit -m 'Initial project structure'

steps.title: Automate Documentation with Claude
steps.description: Use AI to generate a README that acts as your project's North Star, preventing scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional README.md for this AI project, including an installation guide, technical stack, and a roadmap for upcoming features.

steps.title: Implement Feature Branching
steps.description: Never break your main code again by developing new features in isolated branches.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain the logic of feature branching and provide a script to automate the creation of a new branch for my next feature.

steps.title: Establish Commit Hygiene
steps.description: Use descriptive commit messages so you can understand your own code six months from now.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my recent changes and suggest a concise, conventional commit message following the 'feat: description' format.
steps.claude_code_command: git commit -m 'feat: implement vector database integration'

steps.title: Deploy via Claude Code
steps.description: Finalize your project management lifecycle by pushing to a remote repository.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Check my current repository status and provide the exact commands to push my local changes to GitHub.

pro_tips: Always commit before running a major AI model refactor; it’s your 'undo' button for reality.
Use 'Conventional Commits' to make your GitHub history look like a senior engineer’s portfolio.
Treat your '.env' file like a nuclear secret; never commit it to any repository.
Create a 'docs/decisions.md' file to log why you chose specific libraries; interviewers love this.
Run a 'git status' every 30 minutes to stay aware of how much 'untracked' debt you are accumulating.
content.next_steps: Once your repository is live, invite a peer to perform a code review on your pull request to simulate a real-world engineering team environment.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical maturity to manage code like a pro. Stop fearing the 'save' button and start shipping features that actually scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
